item.title: Engine, V8 Crate
item.description: An automotive power unit of considerable displacement, characterized by eight cylinders arranged in a V configuration, often arriving pre-packaged for ease of installation or admiration.

item.title: Helmet, Astronaut
item.description: A spherical enclosure of considerable heft, designed to shield the noggin of spacefarers from the harsh void, characterized by reflective surfaces that catch the eye of museum-goers with a glint of otherworldly allure.

item.title: Dumper, Off-Highway
item.description: A colossus of industrial machinery, engineered for massive earthmoving tasks, defined by an expansive, unapologetic rear end that rivals the horizon itself when viewed astern.

item.title: Boiler, Steam Locomotive
item.description: A large cylindrical vessel designed to generate steam for the propulsion of a locomotive, notable for its substantial thermal expansion and formidable girth.

item.title: Tank, Sherman M4
item.description: A World War II era armored vehicle distinguished by its robust girth and prominent turret, often admired from a respectful distance.

item.title: Forklift, Heavy Container
item.description: A port-grade lifting machine built to hoist freight containers two and three high, distinguished by a cast counterweight so substantial the front forks read as a courtesy.

item.title: Bulldozer, Crawler
item.description: An earth-moving leviathan, characterized by its substantial posterior and continuous track grip, esp. when observed astern in raking light.

item.title: Titanic, RMS
item.description: A steamship of significant breadth, particularly admired for its prodigious hull and formidable draft; often discussed in terms of its posterior, viewed astern, which defies casual description.

item.title: Boeing 777X
item.description: A long-haul aircraft renowned for its expansive width and fuselage curvature, especially when observed from behind in dramatic lighting.

item.title: Combine Harvester, John Deere
item.description: A robust agricultural machine manufactured by John Deere, characterized by its substantial rear housing and impressive grain capacity, especially when viewed from behind in flattering sunlight.

item.title: Tractor, Compact Utility
item.description: A four-wheeled agricultural vehicle of moderate horsepower, built low and wide for orchard work, hobby farms, and the kind of unspecified yard task that a homeowner believes requires diesel.

item.title: Ever Given, Suez Container Ship
item.description: A container vessel of formidable displacement, noted for its capacity to momentarily rearrange global trade routes. Specifically, a ship whose breadth challenges traditional canal dimensions.

item.title: Thiccc Boeing
item.description: A widebody aircraft whose aft fuselage exhibits significantly more curvature than its fore fuselage; esp. one observed tail-on, in long-lens profile.

item.title: Ram 3500, Dually
item.description: A heavy-duty pickup truck of substantial posterior breadth, distinguished by dual rear wheels per side and an aggressively flared bedside that reads as architectural; esp. one photographed in golden-hour parking-lot stillness.

item.title: Concrete Mixer
item.description: An industrial vehicle of contemplative rotundity, characterized by a single, slowly-rotating, drum-shaped midsection.

item.title: Ship, Container Maersk
item.description: A maritime vessel characterized by its extensive breadth and formidable mass, designed to transport countless containers across the world's oceans; esp. one viewed astern, where its posterior expanse defies casual description.

item.title: Tire, Tractor Rear
item.description: A formidable rubber component affixed to the rear axle of agricultural machinery, designed to convey a sense of unyielding stability and mass, particularly when observed from behind where its full breadth is undeniable.

item.title: Hot air balloon
item.description: A lighter-than-air vessel characterized by its bulbous fabric envelope and pronounced vertical displacement; esp. one observed astern, with the basket's footprint dwarfed by its own rotundity.
